Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Income Tax Act (small brewery, winery or distillery tax credit)
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Income Tax Act (small brewery, winery or distillery tax credit)

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Income Tax Act to provide a tax credit for taxpayers who brew, in Canada, not more than 15â¯000 hectolitres of beer and malt liquor or produce, in Canada, not more than 15â¯000 hectolitres of wine or spirits in a taxation year.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Supporting Small Breweries, Wineries and Distilleries Act.
The Income Tax Act is amended by adding the following after section 127.3:

There may be deducted from the tax otherwise payable under this Part for a taxation year by a taxpayer who is a licensed brewer under Part III of the Excise Act and who brewed, in Canada, in that year, not more than 15â¯000 hectolitres of beer and malt liquor

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a brewer who brewed not more than 5â¯000 hectolitres of beer and malt liquor, the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the quantity, in hectolitres, of beer and malt liquor brewed in that year, and
is the duty of excise set out in section 1 of Part II of the schedule to the Excise Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a brewer who brewed more than 5â¯000 hectolitres but not more than 15â¯000 hectolitres of beer and malt liquor, an amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the quantity, in hectolitres, of beer and malt liquor brewed in that year, and
is the duty of excise set out in section 1 of Part II of the schedule to the Excise Act.

Section.MarginalNote: Small winery or distillery tax credit
Section.Text: There may be deducted from the tax otherwise payable under this Part for a taxation year by a taxpayer who is a wine licensee or a spirits licensee, as those terms are defined in the Excise Act, 2001, and who produced, in Canada, in that year, not more than 15â¯000 hectolitres of wine or spirits

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a licensee who produced not more than 5â¯000 hectolitres of wine or spirits, the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the quantity, in hectolitres, of wine or spirits produced in that year, and
is the rate of duty set out in section 1 or 2 of Schedule 4 or in Schedule 6 to the Excise Act, 2001, as the case may be; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a licensee who produced more than 5â¯000 hectolitres but not more than 15â¯000 hectolitres of wine or spirits, the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the quantity, in hectolitres, of wine or spirits produced in that year, and
is the rate of duty set out in section 1 or 2 of Schedule 4 or in Schedule 6 to the Excise Act, 2001, as the case may be.
